blocks.content: There is a video on Kit Jenkins' phone, unlabeled, filed between footage from the festival and a grocery list voice memo he never deleted.
It begins with the field. The April dark, the sound of the town somewhere behind it, the silence of the field. Then the fireflies — a few, blinking in and out.
Then it turns. A woman's face in low light. Dark hair. Eyes that have been crying and aren't quite done. She is looking at him past the glass.
He says: you look — I want to remember how you look right now.
She says: I've been crying for four days.
He says: I know. That's part of it.
She looks at the lens. She is still. She lets herself be seen.
She says: what are you going to do with all this footage.
He says: give it to our kid. One day.
